lines: Chest sits in black mud, too shiny, too still.
Iron lid creaks open, and teeth wait beneath.
Gold gleams inside, but the box breathes slow.
Hummock waits quiet, hiding a hungry mouth.
Lock pops easy, and the lid yawns wide.
Boardwalk creaks, leading straight to riches.
Reeds part slowly, showing a chest half sunk.
Torchlight finds a lid, damp and gleaming.
Frogs go silent, and the chest tips open.
Planks sag under boots near the sunken box.
Mud sucks at boots as the chest gleams near.
Bog bubbles once, then the lid creaks wide.
Chest hisses low, and its teeth start to show.
Acid drips from the hinge onto the boards.
Arm meets teeth, and the trap slams shut.
Dagger finds the seam, prying the lid loose.
Twenty coins spill, then vanish in the mud.
Roll for damage as the mimic bites hard.
Crit lands true, and the chest lid splinters.
Escape the bog before the jaws snap twice.
Mimic sags, defeated, sinking in the muck.
Water closes over planks, calm once more.
Chest jaws hold fast, refusing to let go.
Swamp exhales, and the trap breathes with it.
Iron hinges groan under a heavy, wet lid.
Knees of cypress jut through the black water.
Trail bends past a chest too clean to trust.
Somewhere ahead, a lid gleams like a promise.
Before the box, the mud goes strangely quiet.
Money glints, and the chest edges open wider.
Love of riches blinds you to the moving lid.
Chest sags, half buried, waiting for a hand.
Bound in vines, the chest still looks tempting.
Gone are the frogs, and the swamp holds its breath.
Riches wait, but the chest lid seems to breathe.
Impossible luck rarely comes free in a swamp.
Check the hinges before you touch the gold.
Natural light fades as the box creaks open.
Statistically the mimic wins if you linger.
Ballad of the swamp warns against easy chests.
Glorious gold sits atop a row of hidden teeth.
Splinters fly as the chest lid snaps at you.
Doubled fists slam the lid before it bites.
Even torchlight cannot warm this drowned box.
Undaunted, you shove your blade past the hinge.
Resistant hide shrugs off a weak first strike.
Lunges come fast once the chest reveals fangs.
Grab the hilt and swing before it clamps down.
Box shudders, and gold coins scatter loose.
Seam of the lid glows faintly in torchlight.
Meets your gaze, the mimic grins with wood teeth.
Hisses rise from the hinge as it stirs awake.
Finds you first, the chest lunges from the mud.
Small hope fades as the lid slams shut again.
Don't trust a chest that looks too easy to open.
The lock pops smooth - almost too smooth for comfort.
Gold's shine hides teeth beneath the rotting lid.
It's not treasure; it's a mimic wearing a chest's skin.
Boardwalk planks sag - something waits just beneath them.
The hummock's quiet hides a hungry, waiting mouth.
Twenty coins glitter; none of them are worth the bite.
Iron hinges creak, and the swamp holds its breath.
Check the seam first - it's rarely just a normal chest.
Riches wait ahead, but so do rows of wooden teeth.
The lid's edge trembles, like it's breathing slowly.
Acid drips from its jaw onto the sinking planks.
Your dagger finds the hinge; the mimic finds your arm.
Escape the bog before its jaws snap shut again.
Crit damage lands, and the chest's lid splinters wide.
The mimic's bite lands hard, but you're still standing.
Half the gold's already gone, swallowed by the muck.
It's statistically unlikely, but the chest survives the hit.
Bound by vines, the box still tempts every passerby.
Gone are the frogs; the swamp's gone eerily quiet.
The trail ahead smells like rot and old torch smoke.
Hold your ground - the mimic's lunge is slow but sure.
Course through the mud; the chest won't wait forever.
Defeated, the mimic sags back into the black water.
Small hands shouldn't reach into a swamp chest alone.
Finds you first - the box's lid snaps like a jaw.
Meets your blade with a hiss of splintering wood.
The dagger's hilt slips in your sweat soaked palm.
Fine, take the gold - just watch the seam for teeth.
Twenty steps in, the boardwalk finally gives way.
Love of easy riches is exactly what the mimic counts on.
Money's no good if it costs you an arm to grab it.
Singing frogs stop; that silence means something's near.
The lid's hinge isn't rusted - it's disguised, not broken.
Isn't it strange how clean this chest looks out here.
Lock and hasp gleam untouched by the swamp's grime.
Half sunk in mud, the chest sags like it's tired.
The bog's surface ripples where nothing should move.
Statistically the odds favor the mimic in tight spaces.
Bound hummocks hide more than just loose, wet earth.
The middle of the trail hides the deepest, quietest mud.
Gone, impossible as it sounds, is your easy escape route.
Course correct - the chest's real owner has sharp teeth.
Down in the muck, something shifts beneath the planks.
Hold the line; the mimic's second lunge is the fast one.
Mimic jaws snap - grab your dagger and don't hesitate.
Acid burns through leather before you can pull away.
Arm yourself; the swamp doesn't forgive careless hands.
Down goes the lid, and up comes a row of teeth.
Escape finds you slower than the mimic's hungry lunge.
Small chests aren't always what they're pretending to be.
Undaunted, you swing again at the chest's splintered hinge.
Bites like that shouldn't come from a wooden box.
Crit or not, the mimic's grip is hard to shake.
The mimic has 34 hp and a bite that hits like a slammed iron lid.
Fifty two coins spill out, but 12 of them are just painted lead.
Roll a DC 15 check before you touch the suspiciously clean lock.
The chest's hinge is rusted through in exactly 3 places, maybe more.
Twenty feet down the boardwalk, planks give way over 4 feet of mud.
Its bite deals 2d6 damage plus 3 from the acid dripping off its teeth.
You've got 8 hp left; the mimic still has 21 before it goes down.
The lid snaps shut in under 2 seconds if you don't move fast.
Only 6 of the 40 coins in the pile are real, the rest are bait.
Crit range on this dagger is 19 to 20, and you just rolled a 20.
The hummock sinks 5 inches under your boot before it stops.
The mimic's jaw closes with 14 points of bone crushing force.
Half sunk for 100 years, this chest has learned a new trick.
It takes 3 rounds to pry the hinge loose with a dagger alone.
The swamp's water is only 2 feet deep, but the mud runs deeper.
Forty three gold pieces glitter atop a row of 6 wooden teeth.
Your first hit lands for 7 damage; the mimic barely flinches.
The chest's AC is higher than 15, so that easy hit missed twice.
Five planks in, the boardwalk creaks under 200 pounds of mimic.
It has resisted 4 attacks already and shows no sign of tiring.
The bog swallows a coin every 10 seconds until nothing remains.
You escape with 30 gold and a story about a chest with 40 teeth.
Statistically 1 in 6 chests in this swamp turn out to be alive.
The lid's seam is only 2 inches wide, barely room for a blade.
Nine steps back, the trail forks around a hummock the size of 2 boats.
The mimic bit for 9 damage, then missed its second attack by 1.
A DC 12 check reveals the chest's hinges are freshly oiled, not old.
Its acid spray covers a 5 foot radius around the sinking chest.
The trail smells like rot for the last 100 yards before the clearing.
You've taken 6 damage, but the mimic is down to its last 5 hp.
Only 2 of your last 5 swings actually connected with its hide.
The chest has been sitting there for at least 20 years, untouched.
It swallows 15 gold pieces whole before you can grab the rest.
The dagger's hilt has 3 notches, one for every mimic you've beaten.
The mimic's second lunge deals 11 damage straight through your guard.
Down to 4 hp, you finally land the hit that ends the fight.
The boardwalk has 30 planks, and at least 2 of them aren't wood.
Its bite radius covers a 3 foot half circle around the hinge.
You count 8 teeth before the lid slams shut on your sleeve.
The chest sank 6 inches deeper the moment you touched the lock.
It takes exactly 2 hits to crack the disguise wide open.
The swamp has claimed at least 7 chests just like this one.
Your torch burns for 60 more seconds before the dark creeps back.
The lid opens 90 degrees, just enough to see the teeth inside.
A single 20 on the dice sends the mimic's lid flying off.
Five coins short of 100, the pile was never worth the risk.
The mimic's hide shrugged off 3 hits before finally cracking.
You need 18 or higher to spot the seam before it opens on its own.
The chest has survived 4 seasons of drowned, hungry silence.
One more hit for 10 damage and the mimic finally goes still.
The shrine sinks low, black water rises fast.
Mud grips your boots as the hag watches close.
A bubble bursts, and the frogs go silent.
The hag smiles, her bargain sounds too fair.
Cypress knees rise like knuckles from the mud.
Torches gutter as the shrine door creaks.
An illusion flickers, then teeth appear.
Reeds part slow, something hums below.
The hag offers a deal with a hidden hook.
Rot clings to the altar, sunk in mire.
A soft song drifts across black water.
The shrine glows faint, half swallowed whole.
Teeth flash, then the water goes still.
The hag laughs, and the reeds lean in.
Bubbles rise slow, marking what waits below.
A charming voice calls out your name.
The frogs stop, and the swamp holds still.
Wet steps lead down to the sunken shrine.
The hag tests you with a crooked smile.
Mud swallows your boot, the torch dies.
A shimmer crosses the water, then is gone.
The shrine offers shelter, but at a cost.
Something slips past your leg, cold and quick.
The hag names her price, too easy by far.
Cracks split the altar, water floods in.
A song rises from the reeds, sweet and wrong.
The path narrows, mud grows deeper still.
Torchlight catches teeth among the reeds.
The hag steps close, her smile is a trap.
Six candles flicker low around the altar.
The shrine remembers older, hungrier things.
A friend of the swamp, she says, none believe.
The hag offers a trade, her voice like honey.
Water laps the altar, patient and cold.
Something ancient stirs beneath the stone.
The reeds whisper her name, again and again.
A last chance glimmers before it goes dark.
Dignity fades once the mud reaches your knees.
The hag tests you twice, and nearly wins.
Torches hiss as rain falls through the trees.
A distraction of light dances on the water.
The shrine sinks another inch, deciding time.
Her laugh echoes, frogs answer all at once.
Reeds close behind you, the path is gone.
The hag offers safety, but safety costs.
Black water rises past the altar steps.
A smile like hers always hides something sharp.
The swamp holds many secrets beneath its skin.
Illusions ripple, the true shrine appears.
The hag steps back, testing if you follow.
Bubbles pop near the sunken, mossy stone.
A rude little frog leaps across your path.
The shrine door swings, cold air rushes out.
Her bargain closes, and the swamp goes quiet.
Don't touch the shrine - the hag's smile is a trap.
The reeds bend low; something watches from the black water.
A green hag's bargain always costs more than it seems.
Mud sucks at your boots as torchlight flickers out.
The frogs go silent, all at once, and that's the warning.
Cypress knees rise like knuckles - don't trust the stillness.
She offers a deal; her smile doesn't reach her eyes.
The shrine's stones are slick with rot and old rainwater.
An illusion shimmers - then the swamp shows its teeth.
Gas bubbles burst near the altar; something stirs below.
The hag's voice is honey, but her bargain is a hook.
Wet steps lead down to a shrine half sunk in the mire.
Don't believe the song drifting across the black water.
Six candles gutter around the sunken, mossy altar.
She tests you twice - and twice you nearly take the bait.
Reeds part slowly; the path ahead just disappears.
The hag's smile is charming, suspiciously so.
A last chance glimmers before the shrine goes dark for good.
Dignity means little once the mud reaches your knees.
The swamp's teeth are hidden beneath still black water.
Torches hiss - rain falls through the rotting canopy.
She names her price, and it sounds far too easy.
Something slips past your leg - quick, cold, and gone.
The altar cracks; dark water floods the sunken shrine.
Don't step off the path - the mud here has no bottom.
A charming voice calls your name from the shrine's door.
The hag's bargain closes fast, before you can think twice.
Frogs stop singing; the whole swamp holds its breath.
A distraction of light dances across the black water.
The shrine remembers older, hungrier things than her.
She steps closer - her smile doesn't reach her eyes.
Black water rises past the broken altar's steps.
The hag offers safety; safety always has a cost.
Illusions ripple, then the true shrine appears beneath.
A rude little frog leaps across the sunken path.
Her laugh echoes - the frogs answer all at once.
The reeds whisper her name, over and over again.
Don't trust a smile like that; it always hides something sharp.
The path narrows fast, and the mud grows deeper still.
She offers a trade - her voice is sweet, too sweet.
Cracked stone steps vanish beneath the rising water.
The hag tests your courage with a crooked, knowing smile.
Something ancient stirs beneath the sunken altar stone.
Six sweetlings circle the shrine, waiting for a chance.
The shrine's door swings wide; cold air rushes out.
A friend of the swamp, she claims - though none believe her.
Don't linger near the shrine; the water here bites.
The hag's laugh is soft, and the reeds seem to lean in.
Gas bubbles rise slow, marking something waiting below.
She sings a song that isn't hers to sing at all.
The sunken shrine glows faint through the black water.
Torchlight catches teeth hidden among the reeds.
Her bargain sounds fair - until you read the fine print.
The swamp holds many secrets beneath its rotting skin.
The green hag has 42 hp, and her bargains always cost more than they seem.
You've got 15 feet before the mud swallows your boots whole.
The shrine sank 9 feet lower since you last stood on this path.
Beat a DC 13 to see through the hag's charming illusion.
She offers 20 gold for your name - never take that trade.
The altar has stood here for 300 years, half sunk in black water.
6 candles gutter around the sunken shrine, marking her domain.
The hag's curse drops your max hp by 5 if her smile fools you.
Only 3 sweetlings guard the shrine, but each bites twice as hard.
Your torch has 4 minutes left before the black water reaches it.
The frogs went silent 2 seconds before the hag's shadow appeared.
She's been trading illusions here for at least 200 years.
The shrine's door has 7 locks, and none of them open the same way.
You need 25 gold to buy safe passage past the sunken altar.
The hag's bargain costs 10 hp up front, paid whether you win or lose.
8 gas bubbles rise from the mud, each hiding a small trap.
Cross 30 feet of black water before the frogs start singing again.
Her illusion holds for 6 rounds, then the truth crashes through.
The shrine sits 40 feet below the waterline, cold and forgotten.
Roll a 14 or higher to resist the hag's honeyed offer.
She'll take 50 gold, or she'll take something you can't replace.
The altar cracked into 3 pieces the night the shrine first sank.
You've got 12 hp left, and the hag hasn't even smiled yet.
The reeds hide 5 sweetlings, each waiting for the fog to lift.
Her bargain drops your gold by 30, and your pride by more.
The shrine reappears every 100 years, then sinks again by dawn.
4 torches burn low, casting just enough light to see the teeth.
The hag's smile has fooled 9 travelers this season alone.
Wade through 20 feet of mud before the shrine door comes into view.
Her price starts at 15 gold and climbs the longer you haggle.
The altar's cracks run 2 feet deep, filled with black water.
You have 6 seconds to decide before her offer disappears.
The shrine's stones are older than the swamp by 500 years.
She tested 7 travelers tonight, and only 1 walked away smiling.
The hag's curse lasts 3 rounds unless you break the illusion first.
Black water rises 1 inch every minute near the sunken altar.
You'll need 18 hp to survive her bargain if it turns sour.
The reeds part every 40 feet, revealing another hidden path.
Her song carries 60 feet across the black, still water.
The shrine has swallowed 11 unlucky travelers over the years.
2 gas bubbles burst, and the frogs fall silent right on cue.
You'll pay 8 gold just to hear her offer without a trick.
The altar's teeth marks measure nearly 5 inches across.
Her bargain fools 6 out of 10 travelers who stop to listen.
The shrine sank another 3 inches while you stood deciding.
You've got 22 hp and a sinking feeling about her smile.
The hag has haunted this shrine for well over 400 years.
Cross the last 10 feet quickly before the water closes in.
She'll drop your max hp by 4 if her illusion catches you twice.
The sunken shrine holds 3 secrets, and she'll trade you one.
Torches burn for 5 more minutes before the dark swallows them.
Her price rose to 35 gold the moment you hesitated.
The reeds hide a path only 2 feet wide through the mud.
You have 9 hp, a torch, and one chance to refuse her deal.
Bandages unravel as the mummy staggers closer.
Ash drifts over the sinking swamp path.
Mud sucks at your boots near the coffin lid.
Torches hiss while the wight rises from the water.
Reeds part as something old wakes beneath the muck.
Frogs go silent, and the water stops moving.
Gas bubbles pop near the sunken lockbox.
Black water hides the wight until it strikes.
Rot clings to the bandages like old rope.
Linen strips trail behind the shambling corpse.
Coffin wood splits, and cold air spills out.
Wight claws rake past your steaming shoulder.
Mummy hands close slow around your dagger arm.
Dagger flashes, and the wrapped chest recoils.
Armor rusts fast in this drowned old grave.
Course through the reeds before the frogs return.
Key rattles loose inside the rotted lockbox.
Lid creaks open, and bandages spill like snakes.
Chord of firebolt light cuts the swamp dark.
Music of dripping water hides the wight approach.
Firebolt sparks, and the mummy staggers back.
Corpse hands twitch before the second attack.
Behind the cypress knees, the wight waits still.
Against the mud, your footing finally holds.
Deeper the swamp sinks beneath your boots.
Drops of black water fall from rotted bandages.
Gold gleams faint beneath the sunken chest.
Slippery stones tilt beneath your steady feet.
Steaming breath rises from the wight cold mouth.
Wrapped in linen, the mummy refuses to fall.
Stands the wight tall against the torch light.
Feel the swamp air turn heavy and cold.
Better to strike now before the bandages tighten.
Beautifully timed, your dagger finds its mark.
Coming through the fog, the mummy drags closer.
Dead reeds crackle beneath your careful steps.
Do not let the wight grip your steaming arm.
Each bubble in the mud hides another threat.
Max effort turns the tide against the mummy.
Arrow sinks deep into the wrapped corpse chest.
Finish the fight before the frogs wake again.
Second wave of bandages rises from the muck.
Footnotes of old warnings litter the sunken shrine.
Forgetting the danger, you step past the coffin.
Partner in the dark, the torch keeps you steady.
Pointing past the reeds, the path stays hidden.
Thinks the mummy slow, but its reach is long.
Touch of rot spreads across your steaming skin.
Verse of old warnings echoes through the swamp.
Wonderful timing saves you from the wight claws.
Way through the muck hides another sunken grave.
Meet the wight head on before it grips you.
Save your strength before the second corpse rises.
Turn quickly, and the wight loses your trail.
The mummy's bandages trail through the black water like rope.
Don't turn your back on the wight - it strikes when you look away.
Cypress knees crack underfoot; the frogs have gone silent.
A firebolt splits the dark, and the mummy's arm falls limp.
The wight's grip is cold - colder than the swamp water itself.
Gas bubbles rise slow; something old stirs beneath the mud.
Your dagger finds the seam in the mummy's rotted armor.
The coffin lid groans - and the bandages spill out like snakes.
Steaming breath curls from the wight's hollow, staring eyes.
Slip on the mud and the mummy's reach becomes much longer.
The lockbox key rusts in the wight's clenched, bony fist.
Torches gutter low; the swamp holds its breath with you.
A second wight rises where the first one just fell.
The mummy's linen wraps tighten like a snare around your wrist.
Black water hides the reeds - and whatever moves beneath them.
Your armor steams where the wight's claw dragged across it.
The chord of a warning bell drifts faint across the water.
Nothing in this swamp stays dead - not for long, anyway.
The mummy staggers, but its grip never really loosens.
Frogs go quiet all at once; that's the swamp's only warning.
A firebolt catches the bandages, and they curl to ash.
The wight's voice is a whisper made of old, dry leaves.
Your dagger scrapes rust from the wight's forgotten armor.
Deeper in the muck, the coffin lids all stand open.
The mummy's hand closes slow - too slow to escape, maybe.
Gold spills from the sunken chest, half buried in reeds.
A wight's cold hand brushes your shoulder in the dark.
The swamp keeps its dead close; none of them stay down.
Bandages unravel across the mud like pale, wet ribbons.
Your firebolt lights the reeds, and the wight recoils.
The mummy's bones creak with every slow, dragging step.
Watch the water - the wight moves faster than it should.
A second mummy claws free from the sinking coffin.
The wight's eyes catch torchlight like wet, black glass.
Rot clings to everything here - even the steaming air.
Your blade finds the gap where the armor has rusted through.
The dead don't rest easy in this stretch of swamp.
Cypress roots grip the coffin like fingers holding tight.
A wight's whisper follows you long after it falls.
The mummy's bandages catch fire and the swamp lights up.
Slippery stones shift beneath your feet near the shrine.
The wight rises again - slower, but no less furious.
Mud swallows your steps as the reeds close in behind you.
Your dagger's edge catches the wight's cold, dry throat.
The coffin key turns, and something old exhales.
Bandages hiss where the firebolt's heat licks close.
The mummy's grip loosens only when the bandages burn.
A wight's claw drags long furrows through the mud.
Nothing stays buried long in this drowned old ground.
Your steaming breath fogs the air above the black water.
The wight's second strike comes faster than the first.
Reeds bend low where the mummy dragged itself through.
Gas bubbles pop behind you - don't look back now.
The swamp's dead keep coming, no matter how many fall.
The mummy has 52 hp left, and its bandages still refuse to fall apart.
Roll a DC 14 to dodge the wight's cold, grasping claw in the dark.
Your dagger deals 7 damage, chipping through the mummy's rusted armor.
The wight closes 15 feet in a single turn, faster than it should move.
Only 9 gold spills from the sunken chest half buried in reeds.
The coffin holds 3 wights, each one colder than the last.
A firebolt scorches the bandages for 11 damage before they even catch fire.
Your armor absorbs 4 points from the wight's freezing touch.
The mummy's rotting grip drops your max hp by 6 with every hit.
It takes 2 rounds for the bandages to fully unravel and fall.
The wight has 38 hp, but its cold grip drains you first.
You need 20 gold to buy back the torch you lost in the mud.
The lockbox needs a DC 12 check before the rusted key turns.
A second wight rises after only 5 seconds in the black water.
Your dagger's crit range widens to 19 once the armor cracks.
The mummy shambles 10 feet closer with every slow, dragging step.
Steaming rot drops 3 hp per round if you linger near the coffin.
The wight swings twice, dealing 6 and then 8 damage in the same turn.
Only 45 hp stand between you and the second mummy's grip.
The coffin lid needs DC 15 strength to pry open before the bandages spill.
Your firebolt costs 2 charges but deals 14 damage to the wight.
The swamp path stretches 30 feet before the reeds swallow it whole.
A single torch burns for only 6 rounds in this damp, rotting air.
The mummy's bandages have 12 hp of their own before they burn.
You have 3 turns before the wight's cold grip reaches your throat.
The chest holds 25 gold and a key rusted almost beyond use.
Your armor rating drops by 2 once the mummy's claw connects.
The wight regenerates 4 hp each round it stays in the black water.
It takes 18 damage to finally still the mummy's rotted arms.
Only 7 feet separate you from the coffin before the lid creaks open.
The second wight arrives in 4 rounds if the frogs stay silent.
Your dagger needs a roll of 16 or higher to pierce the rusted mail.
The mummy's grip costs you 5 hp every round you stay too close.
A DC 13 check keeps you from slipping on the mud near the shrine.
The wight's claw deals 9 damage plus 2 more from the cold.
You count 6 coffins sunk halfway into the black, sucking mud.
Only 22 hp remain once the mummy's second wave finally breaks.
The firebolt burns for 3 rounds, enough to melt the bandages away.
Your torch lasts 8 more minutes before the swamp goes fully dark.
The wight strikes at 14 speed, faster than most things down here.
A rusted key opens the lockbox on the 2nd try, not the first.
The mummy takes 5 extra damage once its bandages catch fire.
Only 11 feet of solid ground separate the shrine from the mud.
Your hp drops by 4 just from standing near the wight too long.
The coffin has been sealed for at least 200 years, maybe longer.
A second dagger strike adds 6 more damage to the mummy's arm.
The wight's cold aura drains 1 hp per round within 10 feet.
You need 30 gold before the merchant will part with the torch.
The mummy's rotted grip has a DC 11 escape check, not easy.
Only 4 rounds remain before the second wight fully wakes.
Your firebolt deals 13 damage, enough to stagger the mummy back.
The swamp swallows 5 more feet of the path every single year.
A DC 17 check is needed to resist the wight's freezing touch.
The mummy's bandages need 9 more damage before they finally burn.
Carved stones line the causeway ahead.
Wards hiss along the sunken path.
Malison eyes glitter in the reeds.
Serpent coils tighten near the mud.
Poison drips slow from a fang.
Torches gutter as scales slide past.
Amulet light flares against the dark.
Hisses ring out through the black water.
Green scales vanish beneath the reeds.
Wards crack when the wrong step lands.
Fangs flash where the causeway narrows.
Mud sucks at every careless boot.
Coiled shapes wait beyond the torches.
Priest chants rise over the swamp.
Firebolt sparks against wet scales.
Stones shift beneath the sinking path.
Sneak past the wards if you can.
Malison strikes fast and pulls back.
Cold hisses drift across the water.
Gas bubbles pop beside the reeds.
Serpent glass eyes never blink.
Wards glow before the trap springs.
Scales scrape along the cypress knee.
Yuan ti step from the shadows.
Damage rolls in with every strike.
Attack before the fangs find you.
Slow steps keep the wards quiet.
Twenty paces mark the safe ground.
Mere shadows hide a coiled threat.
Instructive signs warn of poison.
Rude hisses answer every question.
Read the stones before you move.
Untouched mud hides a hungry maw.
Save your strength for the malison.
Resist the pull of sinking ground.
Snake eyes track you from the dark.
Warm torches light a narrow path.
Shoulder deep in muck, you push on.
Between the reeds, something waits.
Detonates the ward in a flash of light.
Black water hides a coiled serpent.
Party of frogs falls silent at once.
First strike belongs to the quick.
Feel the hex settle on your skin.
Every torch gutters at the hiss.
Causeway lined with carved wards.
Set against the dark, the amulet glows.
Music of frogs stops all at once.
Waiting eyes blink from the mud.
Right foot first across the planks.
Cover behind the cypress knee now.
Air turns thick with swamp gas.
Slower steps mean safer ground.
Whole wards crumble under the strike.
Don't step off the causeway - the mud swallows fast.
The malison's wards crack when you get too close.
Wet scales slide past the torches; hold your ground.
Watch the reeds - something is coiled and waiting.
The amulet's glow marks a warded stone underfoot.
Fangs flash in the dark; strike before it does.
Frogs go silent - that's never a good sign here.
The yuan-ti hiss; their eyes never blink.
Careful now - the causeway narrows up ahead.
Poison drips slow, but it's plenty deadly.
The priest's chant rises over black water.
A firebolt sparks against wet, gleaming scales.
Wards flare gold before the trap even springs.
The malison's coils tighten around the reeds.
Don't trust the still water - it hides teeth.
Gas bubbles pop; the swamp holds its breath.
The serpent-cult's wards hum with old magic.
Cypress knees jut like knuckles from the muck.
Slow your steps - the ground here isn't solid.
The malison's hex lingers long after it's gone.
Scales scrape stone; something moves in the dark.
Between the torches, the wards glow instructive.
A single wrong step and the wards ignite.
The malison strikes fast, then slips away.
Read the carved stones before you cross them.
The amulet detonates if the wards are broken.
Rude hisses answer from the black water's edge.
Mud sucks at your boots; keep moving forward.
The party's torchlight barely cuts the gloom.
Something coiled shifts beneath the cypress roots.
The wards feel colder the closer you get.
Don't touch the shrine - the malison guards it.
Fangs find purchase where the mud is soft.
The swamp's frogs fall silent all at once.
Sneak past the wards if you value your skin.
A serpent's glass eyes track your every move.
The causeway's stones are carved with old wards.
Hisses ring out - the yuan-ti are close now.
Whatever waits ahead, it isn't friendly.
The malison's scales gleam wet in torchlight.
Green light spills from the warded stones.
Strike hard; the malison won't give you twice.
The hex settles slow, like cold creeping in.
Torches gutter as the wards start to hum.
Untouched ground here is rarer than gold.
The serpent-cult left its mark on every stone.
Instead of retreating, it coils tighter still.
The priest's shoulder brushes a warded pillar.
Air thickens with swamp gas and old magic.
Save your strength - the malison hits twice.
The wards resist even the sharpest blade.
Whole sections of causeway have sunk from view.
First the hiss, then the strike - be ready.
The malison's wards won't fall to brute force.
The Yuan-ti Malison has 42 hp and strikes twice each round for 6 damage.
Carved wards line the causeway every 10 feet, humming with old magic.
Cross the causeway in under 8 seconds or the mud pulls you under.
The malison's poison bite deals 4 damage and weakens you for 3 rounds.
A DC 13 save keeps you from stumbling into the sunken pit ahead.
This amulet costs 25 gold at the shop, but it blocks 1 hex per fight.
The priest chants for 5 rounds before the wards fully ignite.
Twenty paces mark safe ground; past that, 3 malisons wait in the reeds.
Its fangs have a plus 5 to hit, so keep your guard up at all times.
The swamp swallowed 7 travelers here before the wards were carved.
Each wrong step costs 2 hp as the mud drags at your boots.
The malison regenerates 3 hp per round unless you strike fire damage.
Gas bubbles rise every 4 seconds, marking a hidden pocket of poison.
The causeway narrows to 2 feet wide right where the wards flare gold.
A firebolt for 8 damage cracks the malison's scales wide open.
The hex lasts 6 rounds and drops your max hp by 3 the whole time.
Only 15 gold buys a torch bright enough to light the whole causeway.
The serpent cult carved 9 wards into the stones along this path.
Its bite has a DC 12 save or you take 5 poison damage per turn.
The malison struck first, dealing 7 damage before you could react.
Stones shift every 20 feet, so watch your footing the whole way.
This ring adds 2 to your save vs poison for the next 10 minutes.
The frogs went silent for exactly 3 seconds before the ambush hit.
A malison's coil attack deals 5 damage and restrains you for 1 round.
The wards detonate for 9 damage if you step on the wrong stone.
23 gold buys you passage past the first ring of carved wards ahead.
The priest's chant takes 4 rounds to finish, so strike now.
Its scales resist 3 points of damage from any non magic weapon.
The malison has 38 hp left after your first solid hit landed.
Each torch burns for 30 minutes before the swamp gas snuffs it out.
The causeway drops 6 feet into black water if you miss your footing.
A single malison guards this shrine with 2 lesser wards at its side.
You have 12 seconds to cross before the wards fully recharge.
The amulet blocks 1 hex but shatters after 5 uses in the field.
Poison damage ticks for 2 hp every round until you find a cure.
The malison's fangs sink in for 6 damage on a natural 18 or higher.
This dungeon holds 4 malisons and one much larger serpent priest.
The wards cost 40 gold to disarm safely without setting them off.
A DC 14 check spots the trip wire hidden among the cypress roots.
The malison hissed twice, then struck for 5 damage in one breath.
Its poison halves your speed for 2 rounds after a failed save.
The priest carries 18 gold and a ward stone worth twice that much.
Only 6 hp stood between you and the malison's final strike.
The swamp gas ignites for 5 damage if a torch gets too close.
Twenty stones line this stretch, and 3 of them are trapped.
The malison's tail lashes for 4 damage if you stand too near.
A rope bridge spans 15 feet of black water past the first ward.
The hex weakens your grip, cutting your damage by 2 each hit.
The malison regrew 5 hp before your next turn even started.
Its bite carries a DC 11 save against 4 rounds of creeping poison.
The last ward falls after 3 solid hits from your blade.
Cut one head off the hydra & watch two more burst up from the stump (ugh)!
Roll 2d6+3 against AC 14: can you cauterize the neck before it regrows?
Seven heads snap at once! Do you have a plan, or just a torch and hope?
DC 15 to keep your footing on the slick mud (the mere is rising fast)!
Hit for 4d4+2 and the wound seals - no regrowth, finally, a clean cut!
Bog gas pops around your boots: 20% chance a bubble blinds you for a turn.
The hydra's middle head roars: does it know this fight ends tonight?
Torch damage adds +1d4 fire: burn the stump before anything can grow back!
Twelve necks thrash in black water & only one strikes true this round.
Roll a d20 for initiative: beat 12 and you swing before the swamp does.
Each severed head drops 15 hp (out of 140) - do the math, we've got this!
Mud sucks at your ankles (DC 12 STR check) while a new head hisses awake.
Fire damage (any amount) stops regrowth - cold steel alone just feeds it.
Will the last head fall before the reeds swallow the torch's light?
At 30% hp the hydra dives, and every remaining head strikes at once!
Cauterize now or count on losing ground (each new head hits for 1d6+1).
The swamp holds its breath: can four kids out-swing eight snapping heads?
A head lunges at 90% speed - dodge with DC 13 or take 1d8 to the arm.
Every torch thrown costs a turn: is the trade worth stopping regrowth?
The hydra's roar echoes off cypress knees: fifty feet, still deafening.
Land a crit (natural 20) and sever two necks in the same swing!
Reeds part as the beast surges forward: 60 hp left, heads still multiplying.
Bubble after bubble breaks the surface (something huge moves beneath).
Strike the central head for 3d6: it's the only one that won't regrow!
Cold steel bites deep but two new heads erupt (was that worth it)?
The party circles the mere: who holds the torch while the rest swing?
Ribs show through torn hide at 20% hp - one more good hit should do it!
A neck whips low & nearly drags a friend under the black water!
Roll for damage: 2d10+4 fire finally cauterizes the worst wound.
Nine heads become ten in a blink (this is not going the way we planned).
The frogs go silent all at once: something bigger just woke up.
Torchlight flickers across scales as the last head rears up (here it comes)!
The hydra thrashes, sinking half its bulk (37 hp left and falling).
A gout of swamp gas ignites near the torches: +2d4 fire to every head!
Count the heads before you swing (miscount and you'll waste the turn)!
The beast's tail slams the shore: DC 14 or get knocked flat in the muck.
One clean strike (4d6) finally drops the last head for good!
Water churns black as the hydra dives for cover (60% hp remaining).
The final neck rears up alone: one good hit (3d8+2) should end this.
Every head that falls without fire comes back twice as fast (ugh)!
The mere goes still at last: no more heads, no more heat, just steam.
Roll the last d20 of the fight: beat DC 10 and the hydra finally falls!
Cut one head (2d8+4 damage) & watch two more surge up from the stump!
DC 15 save or the mere floods your torch: burn the wound before it grows!
Roll 1d20+5 vs AC 17 - sever clean, then cauterize (or regret it)!
Every neck you cut costs 10% more hp unless the flame seals the gap!
Four heads strike at once: block 2, dodge 1, and pray on the last (DC 14)!
The stump hisses & smokes - quench it now or face 6 heads by dawn!
Swing true (1d12+3) and the water churns black with rotten blood!
Can you land 3 crits before the fifth neck rises from the muck?
Torches drop to 20% oil: burn fast or the heads regrow in the dark!
Slick scales turn your blade (DC 16 to pierce) - aim for the throat!
Two heads down, four to go - is the stump cauterized yet (check now)?
The beast rears up: 90 hp remain & every strike risks a fresh head!
Hold the line! DC 13 vs the tail sweep or lose your footing in the mud!
Fire (2d6) seals what steel (1d10+2) alone will never finish for good!
Half the heads sleep (40% chance) - strike the waking ones first!
One clean cut, one torch, one silent stump: repeat until the water stills!
